subject: Re: Letter for Jeffrey Epstein
body:
please spend some time and send me your new thoughts,  and expectations of where this is to lead. Any ideas about derivations, 

----- Original Message ----
From: Lesley Groff <lesley@nysgllc.com>
To: J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Sent: Friday, February 22, 2008 2:06:25 PM
Subject: FW: Letter for Jeffrey Epstein



-----Original Message-----
From: Benoit Mandelbrot [mailto:benoit.mandelbrot@yale.edu] 
Sent: Friday, February 22, 2008 2:07 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: Letter for Jeffrey Epstein

Dear Jeffrey,

The first step in selecting something to send you posed a deep quandary
- but "fate" helped. An item that is both very old and very new just
came back to me from the editors with a few suggestions. I forward it as
an attachment - as is, before a few final fixes.

If I seem to be late in responding, it is because of a sudden pressure.
Publishers of a book I have long been working on - and of a proposed
translation of that book - have suddenly decided to have it out for my
85th birthday, one that is scheduled for November 20, 2008. Tight, but I
think I will be done.

Hope to hear from you soon.

All the best,
Benoit
___________________________________________________________________
Benoit Mandelbrot
        Sterling Professor Emeritus of Mathematical Sciences at Yale
University
        75 Cambridge Parkway, Apt E509
        Cambridge, MA  02142-1257, USA
        Telephone: (617)693-1791(office) or (617)620-6598(cell)
        E-mail: benoit.mandelbrot@yale.edu
        Web: www.math.yale.edu/mandelbrot
